events.summary: The Town of Swampscott is soliciting open-market bids for cafeteria floor replacement at Swampscott High School (BD-24-1333-SFAC-SFAC-130425). Scope covers removal and off-site disposal of existing flooring and installation of new flooring per specifications. Bid documents are available on CommBuys; quotes are due June 24, 2026 at 11:00 AM.

stories.name: Humphrey Street Overlay District Ground-Floor Commercial Preservation Amendment

brief.what: A proposed zoning amendment to the Humphrey Street Overlay District in Swampscott, MA that would require discretionary review — and potentially a special permit — before ground-floor commercial space in mixed-use buildings can be converted to residential use.
brief.status: The amendment is at a preliminary discussion stage; the Planning Board reviewed the concept at its June 8, 2026 meeting with no formal public hearing yet scheduled.
brief.whats_next: No formal hearing date is stated in the record; the Board indicated intent to develop draft language in coordination with broader master planning efforts for a future Town Meeting.
brief.outlook: Momentum appears cautious but directional — the Board expressed support for drafting language as early as May and returned to the topic within a month, suggesting the concept has internal traction. Progress will likely depend on how quickly draft language is produced and whether it is tied to an upcoming Town Meeting warrant cycle.

events.summary: Planning Board discussed a potential zoning amendment to the Humphrey Street Overlay District to prevent ground-floor commercial space in mixed-use buildings from being converted to residential use without discretionary review. The discussion was prompted by recent inquiries and proposals to convert corridor properties to multifamily residential. Strategies discussed include requiring ground-floor commercial use in key segments and mandating a special permit for commercial-to-residential conversions; Board expressed support for developing draft language for a future Town Meeting in coordination with broader master planning efforts.

The Planning Board will discuss a potential zoning amendment to protect existing ground-floor commercial space in mixed-use buildings. This is a preliminary discussion with no formal hearing scheduled.
Site Plan Special Permit application to construct an 896 square foot accessory dwelling unit (ADU), breezeway, and porch addition to an existing single-family residence in the A-1 zoning district.

stories.name: 10 Danvers Road Earth Removal / Rock Quarry — Amrep

events.summary: Joint public hearing of the Select Board and Earth Removal Advisory Committee on an application from Amrep for an earth removal permit authorizing continued operation of a rock quarry and associated activities at 10 Danvers Road. The item was scheduled for discussion and possible vote at the June 3 meeting.

stories.name: MS4 Stormwater Permit Compliance Zoning Bylaw Amendments

brief.what: The Town of Swampscott, MA is pursuing zoning bylaw amendments to support compliance with its MS4 stormwater permit, covering parking reductions via special permit, green infrastructure in landscaped parking islands, updated compact-car stall and permeable-paving standards, and overlay-district dimensional changes.
brief.status: The proposal is at the informational stage; the Planning Board heard a presentation from Town DPW and consultant Kleinfelder in May 2026 and expressed general support for the policy direction but requested further review of the redlined bylaw language before taking formal action.
brief.whats_next: The Planning Board is expected to complete its review of the redlined language, with the amendments targeted for consideration at the December 2026 Town Meeting.
brief.outlook: Two consecutive informational hearings with no recorded opposition and a supportive Planning Board signal steady forward momentum; the principal risk is procedural — if the redline review surfaces disagreements or requires additional public process, the December 2026 Town Meeting window could tighten.

contacts.name: Town of Swampscott Department of Public Works

events.summary: Kleinfelder consultants, introduced by DPW Assistant Engineer Mark Noonan, presented proposed zoning bylaw amendments to support Swampscott's MS4 stormwater permit compliance: parking reductions via Special Permit where transit or shared parking is nearby, green infrastructure (rain gardens, bioretention) within landscaped parking islands, and updated compact-car stall and permeable-paving standards in the Smart Growth and Glover Multifamily Overlay Districts. No formal action was taken; amendments are expected to proceed through Planning Board review before consideration at the December 2026 Town Meeting.

Town DPW and consultant Kleinfelder presented proposed zoning bylaw amendments to advance MS4 stormwater permit compliance by reducing impervious cover and promoting green infrastructure. Key proposals include allowing parking reductions via special permit, permitting landscaped parking islands to function as rain gardens or bioswales, updating overlay-district parking dimensional standards, and allowing permeable paving. Board expressed general support for the policy direction but requested further review of the redlined language before any formal action.

Planning Board unanimously approved a Site Plan Special Permit for an 828 sq ft accessory dwelling unit addition and deck at 3 Robin Lane within the Floodplain Overlay District. The ADU is designed for accessible living accommodations to meet a medical need, incorporating kitchen and bathroom facilities, sump pump infrastructure, and drainage improvements to limit new impervious surface. Approval is subject to standard conditions and compliance with floodplain and building requirements.

Proposed amendments to the Swampscott Zoning Bylaw, prepared by Kleinfelder and the Department of Public Works, to achieve MS4 stormwater permit compliance by reducing impervious surface area and promoting green infrastructure. Item is before the Planning Board for discussion.

The Planning Board unanimously approved a Site Plan Special Permit for demolition of the existing structure at 69 Puritan Road and construction of a new approximately 3,000-square-foot single-family residence with attached garage and in-ground pool in the A-3 Zoning District. The project carries prior approvals from the Conservation and Historical Commissions and is designed to comply with floodplain requirements by elevating the structure above base flood elevation. The Board noted concerns about the extent of paved surfaces and dual curb cuts in the front yard and recommended reducing impervious coverage and enhancing landscaping.

stories.name: Zoning Bylaw Amendment – Site Plan Special Permit (Section 5.4.0.0)

events.summary: The Planning Board will discuss proposed amendments to Zoning Bylaw Section 5.4.0.0 (Site Plan Special Permit), including revisions to Sections 5.4.3.0 (Procedures) and 5.4.4.0 (Preparation of Plans) to accommodate online permit applications, and the introduction of new Sections 5.4.10.0–5.4.10.6 establishing an Administrative Site Plan Modification process.

stories.name: Swampscott Middle School MSBA Statement of Interest

events.summary: The Select Board unanimously voted to authorize the Superintendent to submit a Statement of Interest to the Massachusetts School Building Authority for the Swampscott Middle School at 207 Forest Avenue. The SOI cites deficiencies in HVAC, electrical, plumbing, accessibility, fire suppression, building envelope, security, and interior finishes. The MSBA Core Program process timeline extends through approximately 2033.

The Town of Swampscott is soliciting open-market bids for cafeteria floor replacement at Swampscott High School (BD-24-1333-SFAC-SFAC-130425). Scope covers removal and off-site disposal of existing flooring and installation of new flooring per specifications. Bid documents are available on CommBuys; quotes are due June 24, 2026 at 11:00 AM.
Site Plan Special Permit application to construct an 896 square foot accessory dwelling unit (ADU), breezeway, and porch addition to an existing single-family residence in the A-1 zoning district.

The Planning Board will discuss a potential zoning amendment to protect existing ground-floor commercial space in mixed-use buildings. This is a preliminary discussion with no formal hearing scheduled.

Approval is subject to standard conditions and compliance with floodplain and building requirements.
